# UC-10.6.197 · Citrix Admin Console Privilege Escalation Detection

## Description

Elevated configuration work in Citrix environments can change who gets what desktop, what image runs, and what the gateway allows. Monitoring administrative APIs and consoles for bulk or unusual changes from non-service accounts reveals privilege misuse, compromised admin creds, and shadow IT changes that bypass change control.

## Value

Elevated configuration work in Citrix environments can change who gets what desktop, what image runs, and what the gateway allows. Monitoring administrative APIs and consoles for bulk or unusual changes from non-service accounts reveals privilege misuse, compromised admin creds, and shadow IT changes that bypass change control.

## Implementation

Send all admin paths to a protected index with immutable storage if required. Baseline service accounts and break-glass IDs. Alert on first-time admin from a new workstation, burst of publish or catalog changes, or NITRO calls from non-manager networks. Correlate with ticketing; require a change ID in a lookup for expected work. For PVS, track vDisk merge and mode changes as high severity.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites: citrix:broker:admin, citrix:pvs:mcli, and citrix:netscaler:nitro with a common user field; break-glass and service accounts in citrix_privileged_baseline.csv. Step 1: Configure data collection — Full admin categories enabled; admin jump hosts in known CIDR; props for operation, resource, user, client_ip. Step 2: Create the search and alert — Baseline per human admin, alert on acts>5 or rescount>10, first-time client_ip, or NITRO from non-jump networks; join to change_tickets lookup for expected work. Step 3: Validate — Tabletop: approved change with ticket is quiet; unapproved test in pre-prod triggers; run `index=xd (sourcetype="citrix:broker:admin" OR sourcetype="citrix:netscaler:nitro") earliest=-24h` for a drill. Step 4: Operationalize — Route to platform security with quarterly access review; suspected credential abuse or off-hours bulk publish requires immediate SecOps and Citrix platform owner escalation.

## SPL

```spl
index=xd (sourcetype="citrix:broker:admin" OR sourcetype="citrix:pvs:mcli" OR sourcetype="citrix:netscaler:nitro") earliest=-24h
| eval priv=if(match(lower(operation),"(?i)create|delete|assign|full|admin|role|owner|publish|merge|override|masterimage|privilege") OR match(lower(resource),"(?i)catalog|desktop|machine|site|administrator|rbac"),1,0), off=if(match(lower(user),"(?i)svc|script|automation") OR match(client_ip, "^10\\."),0,1)
| where priv=1 AND off=1
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as acts, values(operation) as ops, dc(resource) as rescount by _time, user, client_ip
| where acts>5 OR rescount>10
| table _time, user, client_ip, acts, rescount, ops
```

## Visualization

Table: top admin users by action count; timeline: operations; single-value: admin events with no ticket match in 15m (from lookup join).
